text: Cation exchange effects on nitride phosphors for green light-emitting diodes and white light-emitting diodes Cathodoluminescence and photoluminescence (PL) characteristics of SrxCa0.993-xAlSiN3:Eu2+ (x=0.0, 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9 and 1.0) have been investigated, and great improvement in luminescence was obtained. Their structures and corresponding characteristics were controlled systematically by the cation exchange of Ca2+ ions with Sr2+ ions, exhibiting the influences on the charge balance by generating a rearrangement of nitride clusters in the host lattice as well as demonstrating the impacts on various sites in the nitride matrix. The unit cell expansion, enhancement of the bc plane, and adjustment of the a axis were realized through the cation substitution in SRCAANES. A large amount of high-coordination sites was formed in the Sr/Eu sites due to lattice distortion and sufficient interspace was provided for charge balance of activators in a reduced environment, as analyzed by XANES (X-ray absorption near-edge structure spectroscopy). A more than 10% enhancement in PL intensity and a blue shift of emission peaks were also achieved in the substituted samples. Additionally, HRTCEM (high-resolution transmission electron microscopy) further illustrated the effects of cation exchange on their microstructure. Solid-state Raman spectra demonstrated the changes in the anion environment, caused by a special kind of cation exchange in the nitride system, as well as the characteristic ordering variations of SiN4 and AlN4 units in the matrix. Finally, sequences of (Si/Al)N4 around the divalent sites were further investigated using solid-state 23Na and 27Al nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy. These ordering distributions at the activator sites were responsible for the rigid structure of the host matrix and consequently the enhancement of the thermal quenching characteristics. Thermal stability at 473 K was increased by 10% when x=0.9 in SRCAANES:Eu2+ compared with x=0.0. Thus, the exploration of the neighbor sequence for selective tetrahedral sites (Li2+, Mg2+, Al3+ and Si4+) coordinated by N atoms in contact with the cation sites in a nitride host has been initiated.
